# wrst

> wrst is a framework for building smartwatch apps with React and TypeScript. You
> write React-style components in TypeScript once and run them on both Wear OS
> (rendered with Jetpack Compose) and Apple Watch (rendered with SwiftUI). It
> provides reactive state (useState), effects (useEffect), navigation, fetch,
> localStorage, and live reload during development.

wrst apps can run standalone (a self-contained watch app) or as a companion to a
phone app. Companion mode is designed to work alongside React Native, exposing a
phone↔watch messaging API. wrst is published as the npm package `wrst` with a CLI
(`@wrst/cli`) and prebuilt native hosts for Android (Wear OS) and iOS (Apple Watch).

Status: early-stage MVP. It works end-to-end (development and release builds on
both platforms), but the API surface is small and still evolving.

## Getting started

- [README](https://github.com/karol-bisztyga/wrst/blob/main/README.md): overview, installation, and a minimal example
- [Quickstart](https://github.com/karol-bisztyga/wrst#installation): `npx @wrst/core init my-app`, then `npm start` and `npm run run:apple-watch` / `npm run run:wear-os`

## Core concepts

- Components are plain functions returning a UI tree, written in TSX/JSX.
- State is reactive via `useState`; side effects via `useEffect`.
- The same TypeScript renders natively on each platform (Compose on Wear OS, SwiftUI on Apple Watch).
- Built-in: navigation, fetch, localStorage, a `Device` global, timers, and live reload.

## Companion (React Native)

- Add a watch app to an existing React Native project: `npx @wrst/core init --companion .`
- Provides a unified `Companion` API for phone↔watch messaging.

## Source

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/karol-bisztyga/wrst)
- [npm: wrst](https://www.npmjs.com/package/wrst)
- [npm: @wrst/cli](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@wrst/cli)
